channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Zimbabwe's Businessman Pedzai 'Scott' Sakupwanya: Key Player in Mining and Politics
item.description: Pedzai 'Scott' Sakupwanya, a prominent Zimbabwean businessman with interests in mining, energy, and logistics, has become a key figure close to President Emmerson Mnangagwa. As a ZANU-PF financier tur...

item.title: Rigathi Gachagua's fashion evolution: Embracing casual style in public appearances
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has sparked conversations about his evolving fashion choices, particularly after recently wearing a high-end Rodd & Gunn shirt at a public event. The green and...

item.title: EFCC Says Immigration CG Kemi Nandap Is Not Under Investigation, Confirms Cases Against Some Immigration Officers
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has dismissed reports claiming that the Comptroller-General of the Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS), Kemi Nandap, is being investigated over allege...

item.title: WordPress Vulnerabilities Remain a Major Security Risk Despite Recent Patches
item.description: Hackers are actively exploiting critical security vulnerabilities in WordPress that were patched last week, putting millions of websites at risk. Despite WordPress' forced updates for versions 6.9.0 t...

item.title: BC Ferries reunites lost donkey with owner after community effort
item.description: A stuffed pink donkey named 'Churro the Burro' became an unexpected star after being left behind on a BC Ferry. The incident, which occurred during a sailing from Swartz Bay to the Southern Gulf Islan...

item.title: KPMG appoints John Sams as chief executive after leadership changes linked to audit leaks scandal
item.description: KPMG has appointed long-serving partner John Sams as its new chief executive, formally filling the leadership position left vacant after Andrew Yates resigned in the wake of the firm's audit leaks sca...

item.title: Experts Recommend Age-Based Regulations for Mobile Phone Use in Schools
item.description: The article discusses the recommendations of experts regarding the regulation of mobile phone use in schools, emphasizing the need for age-based policies instead of a complete ban. It highlights the n...

item.title: India's 'Cockroach' Movement Demands Education Minister's Resignation Amid Protests
item.description: India's Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) movement has vowed to continue its protests until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns, following violent clashes in New Delhi. The protests, which began o...

item.title: Court sentences pastor to death for killing disabled daughter falsely accused of witchcraft
item.description: A Cross River High Court has sentenced Pastor Ubong Bassey Etim to death by hanging for the murder of his 16-year-old daughter, Deborah Bassey, who had Down Syndrome. The pastor allegedly killed his d...

item.title: Opinion: Stephen Daisley argues Louise Haigh's appointment signals Andy Burnham's shift towards the Labour left

item.title: Balancing Ambition and Education: Juraysa Athiah's Journey in Modelling and Academia
item.description: Juraysa Athiah, a model from Pietermaritzburg, is making waves in South Africa’s fashion industry while completing her Bachelor of Commerce degree in Information Systems and Management at the Universi...

item.title: Golden Dragon identifies 3km gold corridor in WA with promising drilling results
item.description: Golden Dragon Mining has identified a significant 3km-long gold-in-soil anomaly at its Cue project in Western Australia's Murchison goldfields, linking four previously separate prospects into a single...

item.title: Nyandarua Man Regains Mobility After Funded Spinal Surgery
item.description: Harun Ngarariga, a man from Nyandarua, spent over two decades unable to sit, walk, or sleep without pain due to a severe spinal injury. His life changed after a generous well-wisher funded surgery to ...

item.title: UST Secures Back-to-Back Victories Over St. Benilde in Shakey's Tilt
item.description: The University of Santo Tomas (UST) Golden Tigresses delivered a decisive victory over St. Benilde in the 2026 Shakey's Collegiate National Invitationals, securing their second consecutive win in the ...

item.title: PSX Benchmark Index Surpasses 2,000 Points in Intraday Trade
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) benchmark index, the KSE-100, surged 2,000 points during early trade on Tuesday, reaching 175,927.73 points. This significant gain followed global oil price declines,...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Multi-Agency Online Patrols to Combat Human Trafficking
item.description: Tanzanian authorities have initiated a coordinated online surveillance operation to tackle human trafficking, which has increasingly shifted to social media platforms. The initiative involves collabor...

item.title: Modern Life's Impact on Mental Health: An Evolutionary Mismatch
item.description: A new study published in Behavioral Sciences argues that rapid societal progress has created an 'evolutionary mismatch' between human biology and modern environments, leading to widespread mental heal...

item.title: Family of woman killed in e-scooter crash urges helmet use to prevent tragedies
item.description: Shyan Lolly, a 30-year-old 911 call taker who volunteered at shelters and helped homeless individuals, died after colliding with a vehicle while riding an e-scooter on July 3, 2026. The incident occur...

item.title: Heart Fit Clinic Offers Advanced Cardiovascular Screening
item.description: The article discusses the Heart Fit Clinic's initiative to identify cardiovascular risks through advanced screening methods. It highlights how certain symptoms, such as the burning sensation after con...

item.title: Houthi Blockade Threatens Global Oil Supply and Regional Stability
item.description: The Houthi rebels in Yemen have announced a maritime embargo against Saudi Arabia, escalating tensions in the region and potentially impacting global oil markets. This blockade, if enforced, could red...
